# code-context

> Local code search for AI coding agents: a CLI and an MCP server over an
> index that lives in plain files inside the repo. Keyword (BM25) search is
> live seconds after indexing starts, semantic and hybrid search unlock as
> vectors backfill, and SQL composes ranked search with GROUP BY so "which
> files have the most code about X" is one query. No accounts, no API keys,
> no database server, no telemetry; embedding is a small local model and
> code never leaves the machine.

code-context is built on infino (https://github.com/infino-ai/infino), an
embedded retrieval engine; the same engine and index format also serve
logs, docs, and agent memory.

## Start here

- [README](README.md): what it is, quick start (npm install or one-command
  Claude Code setup via npx), evaluation results, tool surface, CLI
  reference, and configuration.

## Using it

- Install: `npm install -g @infino-ai/code-context` (bins `code-context`
  and `cx`), then `cx install && cx index` in a repo.
- Zero-install for Claude Code:
  `claude mcp add code-context -- npx -y @infino-ai/code-context mcp`.
- MCP tools (stdio): `search` (exact terms AND meaning, one ranked pass,
  hits carry chunk content with path:line ranges), `sql` (read-only
  SELECT/WITH over `chunks(path, start_line, end_line, lang, content)`,
  search functions usable as table-valued relations, `regexp_like` for
  regex), `reindex` (incremental sync; the server also auto-syncs in the
  background).
- CLI: `cx index` (incremental; `--full`, `--watch`), `cx search`,
  `cx sql`, `cx status`, `cx mcp`, `cx install`.

## Evidence

- [Benchmark](docs/benchmark.md): real agent runs vs stock file tools. The
  rule of thumb is that the more a question spans the repo, the more
  retrieval saves: up to 22x fewer tokens on whole-repo questions, 55%
  fewer tokens and 71% fewer tool calls across the codebase-Q&A suite, at
  answer quality a blind pairwise judge could not tell apart. On pinpoint
  symbol lookup, where a single grep is already cheap, it matches file
  tools on accuracy rather than beating them on cost. Includes a
  SWE-bench_Verified localization study and indexing-at-scale timings.
- [Harness](bench/): reproduce every number on your own repo.
- [Embedder eval](docs/embedder-eval.md): how the local embedding model
  was chosen.

## Concepts, FAQ, limits

- [Code search for coding agents](docs/concepts/code-search-for-coding-agents.md):
  the crawl-vs-retrieve model, when an index saves tokens, and where a plain
  grep is still the right tool.
- [FAQ](docs/faq.md): what it is, when an agent should use it, local-only
  guarantees, freshness, the three-tool MCP surface.
- [Tradeoffs](docs/tradeoffs.md): honest limits (no structural code
  intelligence; no token win on pinpoint lookups; one-time vector cost).

## Operational notes

- The index lives in `.infino/` in the repo root (gitignored by
  `cx install`): plain files you can copy or cache in CI.
- Staged readiness: the keyword index commits before the embedding model
  finishes downloading; a failed vector stage degrades to keyword-only
  search instead of failing the index.
- Incremental sync re-chunks and re-embeds only changed files (size/mtime
  prefilter, then content hash); an unchanged tree is a fast no-op.
- Config via env: `CX_ROOT`, `CX_INDEX_DIR`, `CX_AUTO_SYNC`,
  `CX_SYNC_INTERVAL_SECS`, `CX_NO_EMBED`, `CX_MAX_FILES`,
  `CX_MAX_FILE_BYTES`.
